notes:
A test-hardening release. A large coverage sweep across the whole stack —
roughly 11,500 lines of new tests over nine PRs — that, along the way, surfaced
and fixed several real bugs.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Inline images now survive a Markdown export.** Every image you'd added
  (`![alt|width](hash)`) was silently dropped from a Markdown export — and from
  the MCP `get_note` markdown — with no error, losing that part of your note. The
  export now writes the image back out, so a round-trip keeps your images.
- **The open-todos view works again.** The aggregated open-todos list failed on
  every call with an "ambiguous column name" error and never loaded; it now opens
  correctly.
- **"Copy as Markdown" no longer loses blockquote text.** Copying a quote — from
  the block menu, the floating toolbar, or the selection context menu — emitted a
  bare "> " and dropped the quote's actual words; it now copies the full quoted
  text.
- **Pressing Escape while renaming a note now discards the half-typed title**
  instead of saving it. Escape reverted the draft, but the rename committed the
  pre-revert text before the revert took effect — the exact edit Escape was meant
  to cancel.
- **Wrapping a text selection that spans a `[[reference]]` no longer destroys the
  reference.** Typing `[` to wrap such a selection (or pressing Mod+Shift+L)
  flattened the inline reference to a placeholder space; it now wraps the
  selection and keeps every reference inside intact. (Verified on both Chromium
  and WebKit.)
- **Sync no longer risks losing the newer edit in a rare clock-overflow case.**
  At the maximum counter value with a stalled clock, a strictly-later change
  could compare as earlier and get overwritten; the clock now stays strictly
  monotonic so last-write-wins keeps the right edit.

**Under the hood**

- **A large test-gap sweep across the whole stack** — new coverage spans the Rust
  storage crate (export, db, import, ops, apply, history, plus an end-to-end
  Logseq import and database-integrity checks) and sync-core (HLC, sealing,
  key-wrap, wire format, with a blind-server / Privacy-Invariant assertion that no
  plaintext ever leaks onto the wire).
- **Desktop-shell and sync-server coverage against the real thing** — the IPC
  commands, sync, blob sync, alerts, crash reporting, MCP, and the image-protocol
  hash guard; the sync server is exercised against real Postgres (no mocks) — pull
  paging at the page boundary, auth/enrollment guards with no user-enumeration
  oracle, blob-input validation, and admin-purge gating.
- **MCP privacy and rate-limit coverage** — confirming audit rows store only a
  UUID and a length (never note content or query text) and that per-category rate
  limits hold.
- **A stronger sync simulator oracle** — convergence now diffs each block's parent
  and each note's link graph, so two devices silently diverging on hierarchy or
  links is finally caught; new scenarios include a three-device merge and a
  folder-reparent cycle that must degrade to an acyclic forest (the
  runaway-startup class).
- **Broad frontend coverage** across the editor, lib, and components —
  autocomplete, clipboard-paste sanitization, collapse/hierarchy cycle-safety, Tab
  nesting, and select-all — plus WebKit (and Chromium) Playwright end-to-end tests
  for the bug-prone real engine: wrapping a selection over a reference, wikilink
  autocomplete caret placement, and the Cmd+K search palette.
- **Restore-gate hardening** — the database health check wrongly passed for a
  missing file (opening a connection auto-created an empty database), leaving a
  stray empty db behind; it now opens read-only without creating, restoring the
  restore/relocation contract.
